_comment: Hand-built consolidation themes (evals/README.md §3, problem #4). Each theme is a set of linked episodes that should distill into ONE principle. The principle TEXT is supplied explicitly so the eval isolates the consolidation+recall mechanism from the abstractor's text quality (which is backend-bound, out of scope). Treatment clusters the episodes and distills them; we then check whether the single principle surfaces, leads the ranking, and how many episodes it collapsed.

themes.query: how does the user prefer their answers formatted
themes.principle: The user prefers concise, structured answers — short, scannable, and to the point.

episodes.content: The user asked me to shorten my last reply.
The user said the previous answer was too long-winded.
The user prefers bullet points over dense paragraphs.
The user thanked me for a crisp two-line summary.

themes.query: what level of technical depth does the user want
themes.principle: The user values deep architectural reasoning and executable detail over shallow summaries.

episodes.content: The user asked me to go deeper into the system design tradeoffs.
The user was frustrated by a hand-wavy high-level overview.
The user requested concrete code, not just concepts.
The user praised a detailed walkthrough of the data flow.

themes.query: when does the user like to schedule meetings
themes.principle: The user prefers morning meetings and protects afternoons for focused work.

episodes.content: The user moved a 3pm sync to 9am.
The user declined an afternoon meeting to keep focus time.
The user said mornings are best for calls.
The user blocks afternoons on the calendar for deep work.

themes.query: what is the user's stance on testing before merging
themes.principle: The user insists on thorough automated tests passing before any merge.

episodes.content: The user blocked a PR that had no tests.
The user asked for coverage on the new module.
The user requires green CI before approving.
The user values a failing test that catches a regression.
